source_intake_record.raw_question_text: As shown in the figure, in $\triangle ABC$, D is a point on $BC$, $\angle 1=\angle 2$, $\angle 3=\angle 4$, $\angle BAC=108^\circ$. What is the measure of $\angle DAC$?
source_intake_record.raw_answer_text:
\textbf{Solution:} Let $ \angle 1=\angle 2=x$,

$ \therefore \angle 3=\angle 4=\angle 1+\angle 2=2x$,

$ \because \angle DAC+\angle 3+\angle 4=180^\circ$,

$ \therefore \angle DAC=180^\circ−4x$,

$ \because \angle BAC=108^\circ$,

$ \therefore x+180^\circ−4x=108^\circ$,

$ \therefore x=24^\circ$,

$ \therefore \angle DAC=180^\circ−4\times 24^\circ=\boxed{84^\circ}$
extraction_notes: raw_answer_text extracted from solution field; no separate concise answer field
